title: No change: Conductor tells Karnataka transport minister to get off bus
content: Transport minister Byrathi Suresh experienced commuter problems firsthand. A bus conductor asked him to exit for lacking exact change. He also encountered an auto-rickshaw driver demanding an inflated fare. These incidents occurred during a surprise inspection of city bus services. The minister traveled incognito to assess public transport conditions.

title: 9 dead in Pune's Moshi building collapse; waste plant operator chief blames heavy rainfall
content: Seven more bodies were recovered from the collapsed building at the Moshi waste-to-energy plant. This recovery brought the total death toll from the incident to eight people. One person remained missing after the structure collapsed on Wednesday afternoon. Rescue teams worked tirelessly for over 79 hours to locate the missing individual. Compensation has been announced for the families of the victims.

title: FIFA World Cup: England in semis as Bellingham’s double sinks Norway amid VAR fury
content: Jude Bellingham scored a crucial extra-time goal for England's World Cup semi-final victory. Norway took an early lead through Andreas Schjelderup before a controversial moment aided England. VAR disallowed a Norwegian goal in the second half, and England's defense held Erling Haaland scoreless. England now advances to face either Argentina or Switzerland in their next match. The team observed a moment of silence for Jayden Adams before the game.

title: Environmental groups urge FCC to stop Elon Musk, Jeff Bezos, Sundar Pichai's satellite plans
content: Environmental groups want the FCC to pause orbital data center approvals. They are requesting a comprehensive environmental impact statement before any licenses are granted. This action aims to assess the cumulative effects of numerous proposed satellite constellations. Companies like SpaceX and Blue Origin are planning vast numbers of data center satellites. The petition emphasizes the need to understand potential impacts on Earth's environment.

title: PM Modi pays tribute to veteran singer S Janaki
content: Playback legend S. Janaki passed away at eighty-eight years old. Prime Minister Narendra Modi paid tribute to the singer. He called her passing an irreparable loss to music and culture. Janaki sang thousands of songs in seventeen languages over six decades. Her versatile voice and melodies will continue to enchant listeners.

title: Daughter accused of mother's murder for property may have plotted father's death too
content: Police allege a daughter conspired with relatives to kill her mother for property. The victim's brother now claims her father's death also warrants investigation. He suggests the daughter and her paternal cousins may have orchestrated the father's demise. Family members were allegedly prevented from seeing the father after his hospital discharge. Investigators are examining the possibility of a conspiracy involving both deaths.

title: Melinda Gates to students: When a woman steps into power, it is better for all
content: Melinda French Gates received the Josef Korbel Award for her philanthropic work. She stressed that empowering women builds stronger families and nations. French Gates highlighted the importance of centering women in philanthropic efforts. Her organization Pivotal invests in entrepreneurs and advocates for paid leave. She urged students to contribute their skills to local communities.

title: Lionel Messi scripts FIFA World Cup history, becomes first player to...
content: Lionel Messi achieved a historic FIFA World Cup milestone on Saturday. He became the first player with ten assists in the tournament's history. This remarkable feat occurred during Argentina's quarterfinal match against Switzerland. Messi's assist moved him past Diego Maradona on the all-time charts. Argentina continues its pursuit of a fourth World Cup title.

title: IIT Bombay grad: A parent's faith matters more than early judgments
content: A mother's unwavering faith in her son, despite a nursery teacher's harsh assessment of him as 'stupid,' is highlighted in a viral video. The IIT Bombay alumnus credits his mother's belief for overcoming early doubts and achieving success. This story underscores the profound impact of parental expectations, demonstrating how trust can empower children and shape their future, proving early judgments don't define potential.

title: Underwater seagrass meadows could help end global malnutrition, scientists say
content: New research highlights seagrass meadows as vital food sources for coastal populations. Fish from these underwater fields offer a superior nutrient combination compared to reef fish. This finding emphasizes the importance of protecting seagrass alongside coral reefs for food security. Local communities heavily rely on these fish for essential nutrients and income. Conservation efforts must integrate human needs with ecosystem protection for sustainable outcomes.

title: Hours after Zuckerberg posted about AI model on Musk’s X, he gets business proposal
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg announced the new Muse Spark 1.1 AI model on X. X's Head of Product Nikita Bier then offered Creator Monetization for his account. This exchange occurred on Elon Musk's platform after Zuckerberg's AI announcement. The Muse Spark 1.1 model is designed for coding and complex AI tasks. Developers can now access this powerful new AI through Meta's API.

title: Russians urged to work from home amid fuel crisis
content: Russia's Novosibirsk region is urging remote work and reduced car travel. This comes amid a deepening fuel crisis affecting many Russian areas. Ukrainian strikes on oil refineries have significantly impacted fuel supplies. One major facility in Omsk was recently disabled by an attack. This situation has led to fuel shortages and rationing across Russia.

title: India hit the ground running with Kohli, Gill holding a lengthy conversation
content: Virat Kohli, Shubman Gill, Rohit Sharma and KL Rahul were the batters involved in the session, while Jasprit Bumrah, Kuldeep Yadav and Gurnoor Brar led the bowling group. India also used the services of local net bowlers, including two spinners and a fast bowler, to replicate different match scenarios.

title: NHL trade rumors: Canucks eye Maple Leafs' $46.5m winger
content: Matthew Knies trade speculation continues to build around the Toronto Maple Leafs, with the Vancouver Canucks emerging as a team being actively encouraged to pursue the 23-year-old winger. Knies, coming off a 66-point season and carrying a $7. 75M cap hit through 2031, holds no trade protection in his contract. While Leafs management views him as core to their future, rival teams keep calling. Any realistic offer would need to include NHL-ready defensive pieces, premium draft picks, and a top prospect to get Toronto's attention.

title: NBA trade rumors: Lakers emerge as favorites for Hawks' $5m forward
content: The Los Angeles Lakers are reportedly in discussions with the Atlanta Hawks about a sign-and-trade for Jonathan Kuminga, who is said to prefer a move to Los Angeles. While negotiations remain ongoing, the versatile forward is viewed as a strong fit for the Lakers' long-term plans. If completed, the deal could become one of the NBA offseason's biggest blockbuster moves.

title: Jasmine Sandlas announces that she’s engaged
content: During her electrifying concert in Delhi, singer Jasmine Sandlas made a thrilling announcement about her engagement. With excitement radiating from her, she showcased her engagement ring and introduced her fiancé, Shekhar Chaudhary, to the captivated audience. The couple shared a joyful dance on stage as her popular song played, leaving fans both surprised and delighted. This concert kicked off her highly anticipated 'The Dream Girl India' tour.

title: Vietnam tragedy: 15 Indian tourists killed as boat capsizes near Phu Quoc
content: Fifteen Indian tourists died and seventeen were injured when a speedboat capsized near Vietnam's Phu Quoc island. The vessel was carrying mobile phone dealers from Andhra Pradesh and Telangana on a company trip. Rescue operations were launched immediately by nearby vessels and emergency agencies. Vietnamese authorities have ordered an investigation into the cause of the tragic incident. Indian officials are coordinating relief efforts and providing assistance to affected families.

title: Shivam Dube's big blunder turns the game in England's favour - WATCH
content: England secured a commanding 56-run victory in the final T20I match.  Harry Brook and Jos Buttler's partnership proved decisive for the hosts.  India's fielding lapses provided crucial lifelines to the English batsmen.  This defeat resulted in India losing their top T20I ranking.  England completed a dominant 4-0 series sweep over India.

title: Why Bellingham's England goal vs Norway should NOT have stood after bizarre camera cable incident
content: England’s dramatic FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-final win over Norway has been overshadowed by controversy surrounding Jude Bellingham’s equaliser. Footage appeared to show the ball possibly striking an overhead camera cable before England’s goal, sparking debate over the Laws of the Game. Despite the dispute, Bellingham scored again in extra time to send England into the semi-finals.

title: A month after Vizag techie's death at Mussoorie homestay, husband arrested in Delhi
content: Dehradun police arrested software engineer Sri Charan Somayajula in connection with his wife's death.  Gayatri Radha was found dead under suspicious circumstances in a homestay near Mussoorie.  A magisterial inquiry was ordered after the post-mortem failed to establish the cause.  Her parents alleged murder and demanded a thorough investigation into the incident.  Charan was remanded to 14 days judicial custody following his arrest.

title: Karnataka driver kills mom, grandma and brother-in-law; ends life
content: A 34-year-old cab driver allegedly killed his mother, grandmother, and brother-in-law. He then reportedly ended his own life at their Bengaluru residence. The unemployed driver was reportedly distressed over personal issues and joblessness. Neighbours alerted police after hearing screams and commotion from the house. Authorities are investigating the exact motive behind the tragic incident.

title: Gym belt matches Twisha Sharma's injuries, says AIIMS report
content: An AIIMS Delhi team has submitted an 11-page report to the CBI.  Laboratory examinations confirmed skin tissue on the gymnastics belt.  This finding matched the injury pattern on Twisha Sharma's neck.  The report was submitted in a sealed cover under court orders.  This development is significant for the ongoing investigation into the death.

title: Hyderabad horror: SI suspended for not arresting Pocso case accused
content: Family members alleged police negligence in a Pocso case, leading to a brutal murder.  Sub-inspector T Ramesh was suspended, and inspector B Kantha Reddy received a charge memo.  The family also suspects the accused's involvement in the father's death a year prior.  Police stated the father's death was due to ill health and the accused was counselled.  The government sanctioned Rs 5 lakh ex gratia for the victim's sister.

title: FIFA WC 2026: Jude Bellingham joins Maradona in exclusive club
content: In a historic moment for English football, Jude Bellingham showcased his talents by netting four goals in the knockout stage of the World Cup, reminiscent of Diego Maradona's brilliance in 1986. His crucial late equalizer and standout extra-time winner against Norway propelled England to the semifinals, where they will now face Spain. It's a remarkable comeback for England, marking their first semifinal since 1990.

title: US lawmaker Ro Khanna says Israeli settlers armed with US-made rifles detained him in West Bank
content: US Representative Ro Khanna reported being detained by armed Israeli settlers.  The incident occurred while he was visiting a destroyed Palestinian village.  Israeli security forces eventually intervened and dispersed the settlers.  Khanna stated the trip strengthened his resolve for a presidential run.  He also criticized US support for Israel and called for action against alleged genocide.

title: 'Lenin' BO collection day 2: Akhil Akkineni's film records 14% jump
content: Akhil Akkineni's film 'Lenin' saw good box office numbers on its second day. The action drama registered a 14 percent increase in collections over its opening day. Worldwide gross collections for 'Lenin' have now crossed the Rs 23 crore mark. Theatre occupancy remained encouraging throughout the day, especially for evening shows. The film also received positive responses for Akhil Akkineni's performance.

title: Why young Indians are dying of heart attacks despite feeling perfectly healthy
content: Seemingly healthy young Indians are experiencing heart attacks, often without prior warning signs.  Nearly fifty percent of patients with coronary artery blockages show no symptoms.  Diabetes significantly increases the risk of silent heart attacks due to reduced pain perception.  Routine heart screenings are crucial for early detection and prevention of these events.  Awareness and early diagnosis can help mitigate the rising burden of cardiovascular disease.

title: Jos Buttler scores career-best 131, breaks nearly decade-long T20I record against India
content: Jos Buttler's exceptional 131 guided England to a massive 257/3 total. He and Harry Brook forged a record 233-run partnership for the second wicket. This stand became the highest second-wicket partnership in T20I history. Buttler's score was also the highest individual T20I score against India. India faced a daunting target after England's dominant batting display.

title: Ram Temple theft case: Tinnu held keys to donation boxes without approval, reveals SIT
content: An SIT investigation has identified serious procedural irregularities in the temple's donation collection system. Ramashankar Yadav alias Tinnu allegedly controlled donation box keys without formal authorization. Tinnu also facilitated his relative's entry into the sensitive cash counting team. The investigation revealed multiple security lapses and non-compliance with bank protocols. A prima facie case exists against seven individuals for misappropriation of temple donations.

title: India's 1,601-day reign as World No. 1 ends after England complete 4-0 T20I whitewash
content: England completed a 4-0 T20I series sweep over India with a 56-run win in Southampton. Jos Buttler smashed a career-best 131, while Harry Brook's unbeaten 95 helped England post 257/3 with a record 233-run stand. India replied with 201/8 despite half-centuries from Ishan Kishan and Tilak Varma, ending a disappointing tour on a losing note.

title: Claim Hindu law is based on 'Manusmriti' wrong, says SG Tushar Mehta
content: Solicitor General Tushar Mehta stated Hindu law is not based on Manusmriti. Most of India follows the Mitakshara school, which is based on Yajnavalkya Smriti. This school, prevalent since before 700 AD, grants inheritance rights by birth. The Dayabhaga school, prevalent in Bengal and Assam, was more restrictive. Ancient wisdom devised prohibited degrees of relationships, approved by Parliament.

title: After fines fail, Tinsukia films public urinators, puts footages on LED hoarding
content: Tinsukia Municipal Board now displays public urination footage on an LED screen. This initiative aims to deter repeat offenders and promote civic hygiene. Fines previously collected did not effectively change public behavior. Civil defence volunteers assist in recording and displaying these violations. The campaign seeks to encourage residents towards better sanitation practices.

title: 3 UN special rapporteurs flag 'opaque' SIR process; EC dismisses concerns as baseless
content: UN rapporteurs raised concerns about India's voter list revision process and alleged exclusion of minorities. Election Commission officials strongly refuted these allegations, calling them unfounded and unwarranted. The poll panel asserted that the special intensive revision process was transparent and constitutionally sound. They stated ample opportunities were given to electors to challenge any exclusions. The Supreme Court has upheld both the intent and process of this revision exercise.

title: Vietnam boat capsize: Holiday updates for kin back home turn into news of death
content: A sponsored Vietnam trip became a tragedy for several Indian families. A boat carrying thirty-two Indian tourists capsized off Phu Quoc Island. Several individuals from Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, and Kerala lost their lives. Families are awaiting the repatriation of bodies from Vietnam. The vacation was organized by a private medical company.

title: J&K: Kashmiri Pandit's eatery sparks 'ghar wapsi' calls
content: Akash Dhar, a visually impaired Kashmiri Pandit, opened a restaurant in Langate. Local residents and his landlord have offered significant support and encouragement. The eatery has attracted customers from various communities and areas. This initiative hopes to inspire other Kashmiri migrants to return home. The restaurant's success highlights a positive community response and integration.

title: Rotten eggs, spoiled parathas: Swiggy gets 9 notices from FSSAI
content: Swiggy Instamart received nine FSSAI notices for delivering unsafe and expired food products. Consumers complained about receiving rotten eggs and spoiled parathas from the platform. Expired whey protein and packaged snacks were also among the flagged items by customers. The food regulator seeks a detailed explanation and compliance report from the company. Failure to respond satisfactorily could invite action under the Food Safety and Standards Act.

title: Top 5 richest WNBA players in 2026: Caitlin Clark, Angel Reese and more
content: A new era of financial growth is set to begin for the WNBA’s top stars in 2026. Salaries, endorsements, business opportunities: Caitlin Clark, Angel Reese, A’ja Wilson, Sabrina Ionescu and Breanna Stewart are some of the league’s most valuable players. Their soaring fortunes are a testament to the rising popularity, commercial might and global reach of women’s basketball.

title: 'Boat overturned within seconds': Survivor recounts Vietnam tragedy that killed 15 Indians
content: Fifteen Indian tourists died when their speedboat capsized near Vietnam's Phu Quoc Island. Survivors recounted the tragic moments as the vessel overturned quickly amid high waves. Thirty-nine people were on board, with most of them hailing from Tamil Nadu. Vietnamese authorities confirmed the deaths of thirteen men and two women in the accident. Indian officials are coordinating relief efforts for the affected families and survivors.

title: The voice of the south
content: Janaki sang in Hindi and Sinhala too, but it was in the South Indian languages that she carved a niche. Her rendition was infused with love and pathos, and was often a hat-tip to bygone times

title: Kerala remembers the divine voice of S. Janaki
content: Composers, lyricists and singers remember the legendary singer’s enchanting voice, pitch-perfect tonality and enduring influence

title: S. Janaki: Not Kerala-born, but forever its voice
content: With her passing, Kerala mourns not merely a legendary playback singer from Andhra Pradesh, but an artiste whose songs dissolved linguistic boundaries and became woven into the emotional fabric of the State’s cultural life.

title: Naidu, Pawan Kalyan, Durgesh mourn S. Janaki’s demise
content: Chief Minister says the ‘Nightingale of South India’ built an unparalleled musical legacy spanning over six decades; Deputy Chief Minister and Cinematography Minister describe her death as an irreparable loss to Indian music.

title: The nightingale who stole Kerala’s heart
content: Janaki’s effortless singing and pleasant nature earned her enduring love and admiration across generations

title: Police squad attacked in Kozhikode; five suspected goons arrested
content: According to police sources, the confrontation took place at Peruvayal near Mavoor in Kozhikode city around 9 a.m. when the police squad, acting on precise location data, zeroed in on the suspects at a local villa

title: Punjab BJP accuses AAP of subverting the entire electoral process in urban local body polls
content: The ruling party first manipulated ward delimitation to suit its interests, then intimidated opposition candidates, snatched nomination papers and attempted to prevent them from contesting the elections, says BJP State President Kewal Singh Dhillon

title: Mayawati alleges Opposition using Dalit organisations to mislead Bahujan society
content: Congress party hit back adding the BSP chief is reading out from the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) playbook and weakening the Bahujan and marginalized cause

title: Moderate quake of magnitude 4.5 recorded in Bay of Bengal, off Andhra Pradesh coast
content: The quake originated at 05:05:46 IST at a depth of 10 km, with its epicentre located at 16.805° N, 84.381° E, the NCS said. The event has since been classified as “Reviewed” by the agency.

title: S. Janaki: The Isai Arasi who established the voice of the rural, female heroine
content: Janaki amma’s voice gave contour to the heroines, their fears, their desires, their hopes and their dreams, even as Bharathiraja wrote them lion’s space on the screen

title: Congress recalls former NZ PM’s role in reviving ties with India in the 1980s as Modi elevates partnership
content: Congress leader Jairam Ramesh also recalled New Zealand’s role in helping develop the Indian dairy sector, establish AIIMS; says former NZ PM David Lange built close ties with Indira and Rajiv Gandhi

title: CM Suvendu Adhikari lays foundation stone for ₹600-crore Lux Cozi garment hub in Bengal
content: Suvendu promises to create a business and industry-friendly area in Bengal by putting law and order in place and ending the extortion culture

title: Legal questions behind the row over Satluj movie | Explained
content: Can the government legally order an OTT platform to remove a film? What are the legal safeguards? Can the blocking order remain confidential? What are the legal remedies?

title: How undercover agents and informants helped the FBI unravel Lawrence Bishnoi’s gang | Explained
content: A years-long U.S. investigation culminated in charges against 37 people linked to alleged transnational crime syndicates. Here’s how the FBI pieced together the case

title: Boom to bust: Gurugram’s illegal PG business faces crackdown
content: Hundreds of professionals and families living in unauthorised paying guest accommodations and rented 1BHK and 1RK units across DLF’s residential colonies in Gurugram were forced to vacate at short notice last month as authorities launched a sealing drive. With more than 5,000 properties under scrutiny, and thousands of tenants caught in limbo, The Hindu traces the human cost of the crackdown, the scramble for alternative housing, and the thriving rental economy built on widespread planning violations that had flourished for years

title: Poor monsoon and no Cauvery water: distress in Tamil Nadu’s delta region
content: All is not well in the famed ‘Granary of South India’. Between a poor southwest  monsoon and lack of water in the Cauvery, crops and farmers are left high and dry. The kuruvai cultivation area in Thanjavur alone has dipped to around 89,000 acres this season from around 1,94,000 acres in the previous cultivation year.  This report describes the situation on the ground

title: Bihar government launches doorstep land and property registration for citizens above 75 years old
content: Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary inaugurated this new system introduced by the Department of Prohibition, Excise, and Registration. This move spares the elderly the hassle of visiting registration offices, standing in long queues, or undertaking difficult journeys.

title: Committee suggests ₹10-crore project to end undulations on S.A. Road
content: Panel says the gaping undulations were caused primarily by settlement of the road’s 4-lane carriageway; Mayor cites the need for timely disbursal of funds for the project

title: A lullaby falls silent: Kannada cinema bids farewell to S. Janaki
content: Among the tributes that poured in from the Kannada music and film fraternities, one sentiment stood out: her extraordinary versatility

title: Narottam Mishra summoned to Bhopal day after protests rock Datia over ticket denial
content: Supporters of the leader blocked National Highway-44, clashed with the police and resorted to stone pelting and vandalism

title: Chennai Corporation plans holistic infra upgrade for 3 localities
content: The civic body will improve 80 km of roads across Perambur-Vyasarpadi, Ambattur and Guindy Industrial Estates at around ₹1.18 crore; this includes transit integration and junction upgrades

title: S. Janaki’s fresh voice, improvisation made Kerala groove to her tunes
content: Janaki inimitably lent her voice to numerous evergreen Malayalam film songs from the 1960s to the 1990s. Songs such as Manjanikombil, Thenum Vayambum, Nadha Nee varum, Manassiluranroo, Akale Akale Neelakasham, Ilam Manjin have retained their freshness and ability to influence the listener.

title: State seeks assistance from Union govt. for MRTS revamp
content: ₹6,600-crore project is aimed at transforming  MRTS to Chennai Metro standards by refurbishing stations and upgrading trains; T.N. urges Union govt. to consider plan as ‘Central sector’ project

title: AI to help check genuineness of farm data in A.P.
content: The technology set for rollout across the State soon will automatically verify and authenticate images, locations and timelines so as to ensure that field-level data reflects actual on-ground condition

title: PM Modi in New Zealand updates: India, New Zealand elevate ties to strategic partnership
content: An India-New Zealand joint action plan on sport was also firmed up; it provides for collaboration in high-performance sports, sports science, sports medicine and athlete development

title: 8 dead, 14 rescued, 1 missing in Moshi waste plant building collapse near Pune
content: Around 15 excavators, including specialised demolition machines, were engaged in the rescue operation being carried out by the National Disaster Response Force (NDRF), the Army and other agencies, officials said.

title: Families of Vanniyar reservation martyrs also deserve State government jobs, says Anbumani
content: While welcoming the compassion shown by Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay to the kin of the Karur stampede victims, he asserts that such relief must not be limited to one group alone

title: VCK turns its focus to Tamil nationalism post-election
content: VCK founder and Chidambaram MP Thol. Thirumavalavan has urged the party to gear up for a ‘Tamil nationalist’ public rally on August 17 in Ulundurpet near Villupuram

title: When Bollywood jived to S. Janaki’s infectious voice
content: In the 1980s, when there was a massive influx of South Indian production houses into Bollywood, remaking South Indian hits into Hindi, Janaki emerged as a pan-Indian voice

title: V.D. Satheesan condoles S. Janaki’s demise, says she was ‘the voice that captivated generations’
content: Kerala Chief Minister says her contributions to Indian film music transcended the title ‘the nightingale of south India’

title: Four of family found dead at house in Bengaluru’s Kottigepalya
content: Police said their preliminary investigation suggests that Prashanth is suspected to have fatally attacked the three family members before his death.

title: Amit Shah assures church leaders that FCRA Bill will not be applied retrospectively
content: In the meeting held with Mr. Shah, the Minister asked the church leaders to file FIRs in all cases of aggression against the community, urges them to broker peace in Manipur and also reassured them that the Bill was not against any religious community

title: POCSO case registered, after 14-year-old girl gives birth in Hubballi
content: A case under Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012,has been registered in Hubballi after a case of a fourteen year old girl giving birth came to light

title: Mediation helps 15 couple to reunite burying differences
content: Fifteen couples reunited burying their differences during the mediation proceedings at Family court and subsequently exchanged garlands to mark their reconciliation in Dharwad

title: Government intervenes as shipping shocks expose container vulnerability
content: The first outcome of the ₹10,000 crore container manufacturing scheme announced in Union Budget 2026-27 came on July 3, when an India-made EXIM container built by DCM Shriram Group was unveiled at Dadri for Maersk, which has placed a follow-on order for another 1,000 containers

title: Three more held in Bengal lynching case
content: A mob had lynched the man on suspicion of the rape and murder of a 11-year-old girl in Baruipur; five arrested so far in the case

title: Bengal CM meets family of murdered girl, offers compensation to youth’s family in Baruipur
content: The Chief Minister blamed illegal liquor vends and drug-related activities for contributing to the volatile situation and announced for a Statewide crackdown

title: Monsoon tracker updates: Heavy to very heavy rain lashes parts of India
content: In Uttarakhand, heavy rainfall triggered landslides, blocking 120 roads, while around 100 pilgrims were escorted to safety across a washed-out stretch of the Yamunotri National Highway after the route remained cut off for two days

title: State government will release policy to set up ‘innovation city’, says Aadhav Arjuna
content: In his address at the 35th convocation of Sathyabama Institute of Science and Technology, he says facilities must be made available for the students who like to invent, innovate, and try out their ideas

title: One-fourth of India’s monsoon rain evaporates mid-air, says new study
content: Researchers at IITM Pune say 25% of the rain mass evaporates during June to September; this may vary across regions; the finding will help scientists refine weather and climate models and better understand the monsoon

title: NIA chargesheets eight, including Pakistan-based  ‘terrorist’, in Ambala car blast case
content: Case relates to a car bomb blast in the parking area of a police station in Haryana in January; NIA alleges that Shehzad Bhatti had recruited and radicalised several through social media platforms and encrypted communication channels

title: Red Corner Notice issued against Hizbul terrorist hiding in Pakistan: J&K Police
content: Hizbul Mujahideen terrorist Imtiyaz Ahmad Kandoo alias Fayaz alias Sajad, who is the accused in the 2013 Hygam terror attack in Sopore that claimed the lives of four J&K Police personnel, is believed to have exfiltrated to Pakistan

title: Congress steps up Ram Temple campaign, asks Modi to answer for alleged donation theft
content: Congress party holds 26 press conferences across the country, holds Prime Minister Narendra Modi “directly answerable to the nation” as he had taken political ownership of the temple project

title: Almost 6.4% of voter names deleted in draft lists published for four States and one U.T. undergoing SIR
content: Almost 30% of voter names were deleted in Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu, largely because BLOs failed to trace these voters; Odisha had a 6% deletion rate, mostly due to migration

title: Indian-Origin Google Techie Shot Dead By Husband At US Home, Son Injured
content: The accused, 56-year-old Kirk B Wrzesien, was arrested on Tuesday night at the family residence in Cobb County, near Atlanta. He is currently being held without bond at the Cobb County Adult Detention Centre

title: "Now They Pay": US Strikes Iran Over Hormuz Closure, Tehran Responds
content: Iran announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz after firing on a vessel it said was taking an "unauthorised route".

title: US-Iran War Live Updates: After US Strikes Iran, Missile Alerts Sounded In UAE, Qatar, Bahrain
content: Iran-US War News LIVE: The US military has hit a variety of military sites and port facilities after blaming Iran for targeting several merchant vessels off the coast of Oman, sparking Iranian fire then as well.

title: "Unimaginable Tragedy": Lava Mobiles As Indian Associates Killed In Vietnam
content: The victims include 10 persons from Tamil Nadu, three from Andhra Pradesh and two from Kerala, the Indian Embassy in Hanoi said.

title: Iran Shuts Hormuz After Attack On 'Unauthorised' Ship, US Begins New Strikes
content: Iran said the Strait of Hormuz was closed "until further notice" and until "the end of US interference in this region."

title: Delhi Techie Arrested A Month After Wife Found Dead At Mussoorie Homestay
content: P Radha Gayatri (27), also an IT employee living in Delhi, was found dead on the floor of the couple's room at the homestay on June 15. They were there to celebrate the husband's birthday.

title: New Zealand's Space Sector Played Key Role In Chandrayaan-3 Success, Says PM Modi
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi said on Saturday that New Zealand's space sector has played a significant role in the success of India's lunar mission Chandrayaan-3.

title: Vietnam Boat Tragedy Updates: "Boat Had Barely Begun Moving, Tipped Over Before We Could React": Survivor
content: Indian Tourists Killed in Vietnam Boat Incident News Updates: At least 15 Indians, including 13 men and 2 women, were killed in the incident. There were a total of 36 people on board the vessel.

title: Iran Officials Admit To "Mistake" After Attacks On Ships In Hormuz: Report
content: According to the officials, Tehran blamed the incidents on an "errant" faction of hardliners that allegedly acted to derail ongoing negotiations.

title: Man Arrested For Raping 12-Year-Old Neighbour In Assam's Sivasagar
content: The accused, a fast-food vendor from Samastipur district of Bihar, lived in a rented house in the same neighbourhood as the survivor.

title: 2 Arrested After Houses Set On Fire In Manipur, Security Forces Avert Clash
content: The Manipur Police said the two arrested people have been identified as Kammang Lhouvum, 65, and Pagin Hangshing, 30

title: 16-Year-Old Electrocuted In Karnataka, Parents File Case Against Power Board
content: A 16-year-old boy allegedly died of electrocution after coming into contact with a low-hanging electric wire connected to a government school in Karnataka's Tumakuru district.

title: Bengaluru Woman Says Delivery Agent Barged Into Home To Use Toilet, Flashed Her
content: She said the delivery agent had come to her house to deliver a parcel and asked her for permission to use the washroom. When she refused, he forcibly entered the house.

title: 5 Killed Inside Well Due To Toxic Fumes From Generator In Shillong
content: Four workers and a local resident were killed due to toxic fumes from a generator while working inside a well

title: Boat Travelled Barely 300 Metres Before Toppling: Indian Witness In Vietnam
content: A boat carrying a group of Indian tourists that capsized off an island in south Vietnam on Saturday had barely travelled 300-400 metres from the shore when it suddenly overturned, an Indian national who witnessed the tragedy said.

title: 'Compromised Leaders": Congress Sees Infighting Over Replacing Punjab Chief
content: On July 1, the Congress announced that Amrinder Singh RajaWarring would continue as the Punjab unit president and appointed Charanjit Singh Channi as chairperson of the campaign committee.

title: 15 Indian Tourists Killed As Boat Overturns In Vietnam
content: Search and rescue operations are underway, and the Indian mission has set up control rooms to assist those affected.

title: Some Jumped Out, I Saw Him Get Stuck: Friend Of Man Killed In Vietnam Tragedy
content: Nirmal Kumar, one of the survivors, said the vessel was a closed speedboat carrying 32 passengers and that it overturned within three minutes of setting off.

title: S Janaki, the voice of south Indian cinema, passes away at 88
content: Neeraja Ramesh is a Chennai-based journalist with The Times of India. As senior news editor, she heads the Tamil Nadu nation desk and oversees city and front pages of TN editions. With 25 years of experience in journalism, she has in-depth knowledge in cinema, trends, art and culture. Read More

title: India, New Zealand upgrade their 'made for each other' ties
content: india and new Zealand signed defence agreements to boost maritime cooperation in the Indo-Pacific, facilitate reciprocal logistics support between the Indian Navy and the New Zealand Defence Force and strengthen hydrographic collaboration . modi described the two countries as "natural partners" that are "made for each other"

title: Speedy completion of UAPA trials a challenge, says CJI Suryakant
content: chief justice Surya Kant says expeditious completion of trial in these cases is desirable . he says final adjudication of evidence would address grievances of non-grant of bail despite long period of incarceration .

title: Rajasthan: Maternal mortality crisis worsens as 9 more women die
content: nine women have died in government-run hospitals across five districts . the deaths are the latest in a string of similar incidents reported earlier from Kota, Bikaner and Jodhpur since may this year . five new deaths were reported from the Mahatma Gandhi Hospital between July 5 and July 10 .

title: Hyderabad horror: Pocso accused kills 6, including 16-yr-old girl, in overnight rampage
content: He has been reporting crime, courts, politics, transport, and civic issues for 18 years. Currently working for The Times of India as an Assistant Editor in Hyderabad.
